forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,52 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9880'
+project: op-token-diet
+title: Heavy task mutations return thin ack {ok,id,short_id,changed}; add/paste return slim task
+---
+## What
+Five task mutation ops in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/` end their `execute` with `Ok(task_entity_to_json(&entity))` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task_helpers.rs`), echoing the FULL task ŌĆö `description`, `attachments`, every field ŌĆö back to the agent that already has it. An `update task` on a long card echoes kilobytes per call, every call. Replace those responses:
+
+**Thin acknowledgment** ŌĆö `update.rs` (`UpdateTask`), `mv.rs` (`MoveTask`), `complete.rs` (`CompleteTask`) return:
+```json
+{ "ok": true, "id": "<ulid>", "short_id": "<7-char>", "changed": { "<field>": <new stored value>, ... } }
+```
+- `changed` carries exactly the fields the op modified, with the STORED (post-normalization) values ŌĆö event-FieldChange style. Useful because stored can differ from input: dates normalized to `YYYY-MM-DD` (`apply_optional_date`), move/complete ordinals computed server-side.
+- `update`: derive `changed` from the same `Some`-field checks as `UpdateTask::apply_to` (title, description, assignees, depends_on, attachments, project, due, scheduled). Yes, a changed description is echoed in `changed` ŌĆö bounded by what the caller just sent and only on ops that touched it; uniformity beats a special case.
+- `move`: `changed: { "position": { "column", "ordinal" } }` ŌĆö the server-computed ordinal is the information the agent lacks.
+- `complete`: `changed: { "position": { "column", "ordinal" } }` (terminal column + computed end-of-column ordinal).
+
+**Slim projection for creations** ŌĆö `add.rs` (`AddTask`) and `paste.rs` (`PasteTask`) return `slim_task_json(...)` (the allowlist projection introduced by card ^624prsf): the agent needs `id`/`short_id`/`position` it didn't have, but not its own description/attachments echoed back.
+
+Implementation:
+- Add `pub(crate) fn task_mutation_ack(entity: &Entity, changed: serde_json::Map<String, Value>) -> Value` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task_helpers.rs` next to `task_entity_to_json` ŌĆö single source of the envelope; derives `short_id` via `crate::types::short_id`.
+- HARD CONSTRAINT: top-level `id` must remain ŌĆö the MCP wrapper `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs` reads `result["id"]` (in `KanbanTool::call`, near `should_include_plan`) to set `_plan._meta.affected_task_id`. The `_plan` attachment itself is at the tools layer and is untouched by this card.
+- `get task` (`get.rs`) and `next task` (`next.rs`) stay FULL (`task_entity_to_rich_json`) ŌĆö the agreed escape hatch; want the full card after mutating ŌåÆ call `get task`.
+- NO `detail` param on mutations (decided). Alternatives weighed and rejected: (a) slim projection on every mutation ŌĆö still echoes ~15 fields the agent already knows and answers "what is it" not "what changed"; (b) `detail` param defaulting to thin ŌĆö extra API surface duplicating `get task`.
+- Consumers verified: kanban-cli (`apps/kanban-cli/src/main.rs::execute_kanban_operation`, `commands/serve.rs`) prints whatever YAML/JSON comes back ŌĆö no field assumptions; desktop UI consumes broadcast events through the entity store, never op responses; skill prose (builtin/skills/plan/references/PLANNING_GUIDE.md) only captures the returned task id from `add task`, which the slim projection keeps.
+
+Test migration (the real blast radius ŌĆö tests asserting the old full shape on mutation responses):
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: asserts like `result["title"]`, `result["description"]`, `result["position"]["column"]` directly on add/update/move/complete results (e.g. updateŌåÆ"New desc", moveŌåÆposition asserts). Re-point shape asserts at the ack/slim shape; where a test needs full fields, fetch via `get task`.
+- Per-op `#[cfg(test)]` modules: `add.rs` (asserts `result["description"]`, system-date checks), `update.rs`, `mv.rs`, `complete.rs`, `paste.rs`.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs` tests: asserts on `data["title"]`/`data["position"]["column"]` after add/update/move/complete.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `update task`, `move task`, `complete task` responses contain exactly `ok`, `id`, `short_id`, `changed` ŌĆö no `description`/`attachments`/`title` echo unless that field was changed by the op.
+- [ ] `update task` setting only `title` yields `changed == {"title": <new>}`; setting `due` yields the normalized `YYYY-MM-DD` stored value in `changed`.
+- [ ] `move task` / `complete task` report the server-computed `position.ordinal` in `changed`.
+- [ ] `add task` / `paste` return the slim projection (allowlist fields incl. `id`, `short_id`, `position`; NO `description`/`attachments` keys).
+- [ ] MCP kanban tool still attaches `_plan` with `affected_task_id` populated from mutation responses.
+- [ ] `get task` and `next task` responses unchanged (full rich JSON, description present).
+- [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p swissarmyhammer-tools -- -D warnings` clean.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `task_helpers.rs` unit test for `task_mutation_ack`: envelope has exactly ok/id/short_id/changed; short_id derived from the ULID.
+- [ ] Op tests (TempDir board pattern: `InitBoard` + `AddTask`) in `update.rs`, `mv.rs`, `complete.rs`: assert the exact response key set and the absence of `description`; `add.rs`/`paste.rs`: response is slim (no `description`/`attachments` keys, has `position`).
+- [ ] `dispatch.rs` integration tests migrated: add ŌåÆ update(title) asserts ack shape; move asserts `changed.position`; full-field asserts go through `get task`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools` kanban tool test: `update task` response carries `_plan._meta.affected_task_id` equal to the mutated task id.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p swissarmyhammer-tools` ŌĆö green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the ack-shape tests first (red), then implement `task_mutation_ack` and rewire the five ops.
\ No newline at end of file

 Five task mutation ops in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/` end their `execute` with `Ok(task_entity_to_json(&entity))` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task_helpers.rs`), echoing the FULL task ŌĆö `description`, `attachments`, every field ŌĆö back to the agent that already has it. -**Thin acknowledgment** ŌĆö `update.rs` (`UpdateTask`), `mv.rs` (`MoveTask`), `complete.rs` (`CompleteTask`) return:
+**Pure identity acknowledgment** ŌĆö `update.rs` (`UpdateTask`), `mv.rs` (`MoveTask`), `complete.rs` (`CompleteTask`) return:
 ```json
-{ "ok": true, "id": "<ulid>", "short_id": "<7-char>", "changed": { "<field>": <new stored value>, ... } }
+{ "ok": true, "id": "<ulid>", "short_id": "<7-char>" }
 ```
-- `changed` carries exactly the fields the op modified, with the STORED (post-normalization) values ŌĆö event-FieldChange style. Useful because stored can differ from input: dates normalized to `YYYY-MM-DD` (`apply_optional_date`), move/complete ordinals computed server-side.
-- `update`: derive `changed` from the same `Some`-field checks as `UpdateTask::apply_to` (title, description, assignees, depends_on, attachments, project, due, scheduled). Yes, a changed description is echoed in `changed` ŌĆö bounded by what the caller just sent and only on ops that touched it; uniformity beats a special case.
-- `move`: `changed: { "position": { "column", "ordinal" } }` ŌĆö the server-computed ordinal is the information the agent lacks.
-- `complete`: `changed: { "position": { "column", "ordinal" } }` (terminal column + computed end-of-column ordinal).
+- NO `changed` field map (DECIDED ŌĆö rejected): echoing changed-field VALUES re-pays for the same tokens the agent just sent ŌĆö an updated description/body would be billed twice. Server-computed values (normalized `YYYY-MM-DD` dates from `apply_optional_date`, move/complete ordinals) are not worth a special case either: the agent asked for the move/date, success implies it took effect, and `get task` is the escape hatch on the rare occasion the stored value matters.
+- The mutation either fully succeeds (ack) or errors (KanbanError) ŌĆö there is no partial-success state the response needs to describe.

 **Slim projection for creations** ŌĆö `add.rs` (`AddTask`) and `paste.rs` (`PasteTask`) return `slim_task_json(...)` (the allowlist projection introduced by card ^624prsf): the agent needs `id`/`short_id`/`position` it didn't have, but not its own description/attachments echoed back.

 Implementation:
-- Add `pub(crate) fn task_mutation_ack(entity: &Entity, changed: serde_json::Map<String, Value>) -> Value` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task_helpers.rs` next to `task_entity_to_json` ŌĆö single source of the envelope; derives `short_id` via `crate::types::short_id`.

 - HARD CONSTRAINT: top-level `id` must remain ŌĆö the MCP wrapper `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs` reads `result["id"]` (in `KanbanTool::call`, near `should_include_plan`) to set `_plan._meta.affected_task_id`. The `_plan` attachment itself is at the tools layer and is untouched by this card.
 - `get task` (`get.rs`) and `next task` (`next.rs`) stay FULL (`task_entity_to_rich_json`) ŌĆö the agreed escape hatch; want the full card after mutating ŌåÆ call `get task`.
-- NO `detail` param on mutations (decided). Alternatives weighed and rejected: (a) slim projection on every mutation ŌĆö still echoes ~15 fields the agent already knows and answers "what is it" not "what changed"; (b) `detail` param defaulting to thin ŌĆö extra API surface duplicating `get task`.
+- NO `detail` param on mutations (decided). Alternatives weighed and rejected: (a) slim projection on every mutation ŌĆö still echoes ~15 fields the agent already knows; (b) `detail` param defaulting to thin ŌĆö extra API surface duplicating `get task`; (c) `changed` field/value map ŌĆö re-echoes the very payload the agent just sent (see above).
 - Consumers verified: kanban-cli (`apps/kanban-cli/src/main.rs::execute_kanban_operation`, `commands/serve.rs`) prints whatever YAML/JSON comes back ŌĆö no field assumptions; desktop UI consumes broadcast events through the entity store, never op responses; skill prose (builtin/skills/plan/references/PLANNING_GUIDE.md) only captures the returned task id from `add task`, which the slim projection keeps.

 Test migration (the real blast radius ŌĆö tests asserting the old full shape on mutation responses):
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: asserts like `result["title"]`, `result["description"]`, `result["position"]["column"]` directly on add/update/move/complete results (e.g. updateŌåÆ"New desc", moveŌåÆposition asserts). Re-point shape asserts at the ack/slim shape; where a test needs full fields, fetch via `get task`.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: asserts like `result["title"]`, `result["description"]`, `result["position"]["column"]` directly on add/update/move/complete results (e.g. updateŌåÆ"New desc", moveŌåÆposition asserts). Re-point shape asserts at the ack/slim shape; where a test needs to verify the mutation's EFFECT (new title, new position, normalized date), fetch via `get task` and assert there ŌĆö that's also the more honest test (asserts stored state, not response echo).
 - Per-op `#[cfg(test)]` modules: `add.rs` (asserts `result["description"]`, system-date checks), `update.rs`, `mv.rs`, `complete.rs`, `paste.rs`.
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs` tests: asserts on `data["title"]`/`data["position"]["column"]` after add/update/move/complete.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `update task`, `move task`, `complete task` responses contain exactly `ok`, `id`, `short_id`, `changed` ŌĆö no `description`/`attachments`/`title` echo unless that field was changed by the op.
-- [ ] `update task` setting only `title` yields `changed == {"title": <new>}`; setting `due` yields the normalized `YYYY-MM-DD` stored value in `changed`.
-- [ ] `move task` / `complete task` report the server-computed `position.ordinal` in `changed`.
+- [ ] `update task`, `move task`, `complete task` responses contain exactly `ok`, `id`, `short_id` ŌĆö nothing else; no field echo of any kind.
 - [ ] `add task` / `paste` return the slim projection (allowlist fields incl. `id`, `short_id`, `position`; NO `description`/`attachments` keys).
+- [ ] Mutation effects are still fully verified: tests assert post-op state via `get task` (e.g. update title ŌåÆ get task shows new title; move ŌåÆ get task shows new column/ordinal; due date ŌåÆ normalized `YYYY-MM-DD`).
 - [ ] MCP kanban tool still attaches `_plan` with `affected_task_id` populated from mutation responses.
 - [ ] `get task` and `next task` responses unchanged (full rich JSON, description present).
 - [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p swissarmyhammer-tools -- -D warnings` clean.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `task_helpers.rs` unit test for `task_mutation_ack`: envelope has exactly ok/id/short_id/changed; short_id derived from the ULID.
-- [ ] Op tests (TempDir board pattern: `InitBoard` + `AddTask`) in `update.rs`, `mv.rs`, `complete.rs`: assert the exact response key set and the absence of `description`; `add.rs`/`paste.rs`: response is slim (no `description`/`attachments` keys, has `position`).
-- [ ] `dispatch.rs` integration tests migrated: add ŌåÆ update(title) asserts ack shape; move asserts `changed.position`; full-field asserts go through `get task`.
+- [ ] `task_helpers.rs` unit test for `task_mutation_ack`: envelope has exactly ok/id/short_id; short_id derived from the ULID.
+- [ ] Op tests (TempDir board pattern: `InitBoard` + `AddTask`) in `update.rs`, `mv.rs`, `complete.rs`: assert the exact three-key response set and the absence of `description`/`title`/`position`; then `get task` asserts the stored effect. `add.rs`/`paste.rs`: response is slim (no `description`/`attachments` keys, has `position`).
+- [ ] `dispatch.rs` integration tests migrated: add ŌåÆ update(title) asserts ack shape; effect asserts go through `get task`.
 - [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools` kanban tool test: `update task` response carries `_plan._meta.affected_task_id` equal to the mutated task id.
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p swissarmyhammer-tools` ŌĆö green.

-- Add `pub(crate) fn task_mutation_ack(entity: &Entity) -> Value` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task_helpers.rs` next to `task_entity_to_json` ŌĆö single source of the envelope; derives `short_id` via `crate::types::short_id`.
+- Add `pub(crate) fn task_mutation_ack(entity: &Entity, changed: serde_json::Map<String, Value>) -> Value` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task_helpers.rs` next to `task_entity_to_json` ŌĆö single source of the envelope; derives `short_id` via `crate::types::short_id`.
 - HARD CONSTRAINT: top-level `id` must remain ŌĆö the MCP wrapper `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs` reads `result["id"]` (in `KanbanTool::call`, near `should_include_plan`) to set `_plan._meta.affected_task_id`. The `_plan` attachment itself is at the tools layer and is untouched by this card.
 - `get task` (`get.rs`) and `next task` (`next.rs`) stay FULL (`task_entity_to_rich_json`) ŌĆö the agreed escape hatch; want the full card after mutating ŌåÆ call `get task`.
-- NO `detail` param on mutations (decided). Alternatives weighed and rejected: (a) slim projection on every mutation ŌĆö still echoes ~15 fields the agent already knows; (b) `detail` param defaulting to thin ŌĆö extra API surface duplicating `get task`; (c) `changed` field/value map ŌĆö re-echoes the very payload the agent just sent (see above).
+- NO `detail` param on mutations (decided). Alternatives weighed and rejected: (a) slim projection on every mutation ŌĆö still echoes ~15 fields the agent already knows and answers "what is it" not "what changed"; (b) `detail` param defaulting to thin ŌĆö extra API surface duplicating `get task`.
 - Consumers verified: kanban-cli (`apps/kanban-cli/src/main.rs::execute_kanban_operation`, `commands/serve.rs`) prints whatever YAML/JSON comes back ŌĆö no field assumptions; desktop UI consumes broadcast events through the entity store, never op responses; skill prose (builtin/skills/plan/references/PLANNING_GUIDE.md) only captures the returned task id from `add task`, which the slim projection keeps.

 Test migration (the real blast radius ŌĆö tests asserting the old full shape on mutation responses):
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: asserts like `result["title"]`, `result["description"]`, `result["position"]["column"]` directly on add/update/move/complete results (e.g. updateŌåÆ"New desc", moveŌåÆposition asserts). Re-point shape asserts at the ack/slim shape; where a test needs to verify the mutation's EFFECT (new title, new position, normalized date), fetch via `get task` and assert there ŌĆö that's also the more honest test (asserts stored state, not response echo).
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: asserts like `result["title"]`, `result["description"]`, `result["position"]["column"]` directly on add/update/move/complete results (e.g. updateŌåÆ"New desc", moveŌåÆposition asserts). Re-point shape asserts at the ack/slim shape; where a test needs full fields, fetch via `get task`.
 - Per-op `#[cfg(test)]` modules: `add.rs` (asserts `result["description"]`, system-date checks), `update.rs`, `mv.rs`, `complete.rs`, `paste.rs`.
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs` tests: asserts on `data["title"]`/`data["position"]["column"]` after add/update/move/complete.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `update task`, `move task`, `complete task` responses contain exactly `ok`, `id`, `short_id` ŌĆö nothing else; no field echo of any kind.
+- [ ] `update task`, `move task`, `complete task` responses contain exactly `ok`, `id`, `short_id`, `changed` ŌĆö no `description`/`attachments`/`title` echo unless that field was changed by the op.
+- [ ] `update task` setting only `title` yields `changed == {"title": <new>}`; setting `due` yields the normalized `YYYY-MM-DD` stored value in `changed`.
+- [ ] `move task` / `complete task` report the server-computed `position.ordinal` in `changed`.
 - [ ] `add task` / `paste` return the slim projection (allowlist fields incl. `id`, `short_id`, `position`; NO `description`/`attachments` keys).
-- [ ] Mutation effects are still fully verified: tests assert post-op state via `get task` (e.g. update title ŌåÆ get task shows new title; move ŌåÆ get task shows new column/ordinal; due date ŌåÆ normalized `YYYY-MM-DD`).
 - [ ] MCP kanban tool still attaches `_plan` with `affected_task_id` populated from mutation responses.
 - [ ] `get task` and `next task` responses unchanged (full rich JSON, description present).
 - [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p swissarmyhammer-tools -- -D warnings` clean.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `task_helpers.rs` unit test for `task_mutation_ack`: envelope has exactly ok/id/short_id; short_id derived from the ULID.
-- [ ] Op tests (TempDir board pattern: `InitBoard` + `AddTask`) in `update.rs`, `mv.rs`, `complete.rs`: assert the exact three-key response set and the absence of `description`/`title`/`position`; then `get task` asserts the stored effect. `add.rs`/`paste.rs`: response is slim (no `description`/`attachments` keys, has `position`).
-- [ ] `dispatch.rs` integration tests migrated: add ŌåÆ update(title) asserts ack shape; effect asserts go through `get task`.
+- [ ] `task_helpers.rs` unit test for `task_mutation_ack`: envelope has exactly ok/id/short_id/changed; short_id derived from the ULID.
+- [ ] Op tests (TempDir board pattern: `InitBoard` + `AddTask`) in `update.rs`, `mv.rs`, `complete.rs`: assert the exact response key set and the absence of `description`; `add.rs`/`paste.rs`: response is slim (no `description`/`attachments` keys, has `position`).
+- [ ] `dispatch.rs` integration tests migrated: add ŌåÆ update(title) asserts ack shape; move asserts `changed.position`; full-field asserts go through `get task`.
 - [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools` kanban tool test: `update task` response carries `_plan._meta.affected_task_id` equal to the mutated task id.
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p swissarmyhammer-tools` ŌĆö green.
